board.title: Pioneers of Computing
board.author: Julius Boateng
board.description: The people in this puzzle helped shape modern computing. They did not work alone; many unsung contributors helped advance the field.

clues.question: Introduced semaphores for mutual exclusion in concurrent programming; developed a classic shortest-path algorithm
Developed the first compiler; helped pioneer machine-independent programming languages such as COBOL
Designed the Analytical Engine, an early general-purpose programmable computer
Pioneered the mathematical analysis of algorithms; author of "The Art of Computer Programming"
Proposed the stored-program architecture used by most modern computers; ___ architecture
Invented Quicksort; developed Hoare logic for reasoning about program correctness
Proposed the relational database model in the seminal 1970 paper "A Relational Model of Data for Large Shared Data Banks"
Pioneered dynamic instruction scheduling in IBM processors; later co-developed modern VLSI chip design methods
Applied Boolean algebra to switching circuits; founded information theory in the 1948 paper "A Mathematical Theory of Communication"
Mathematician who helped break the Enigma cipher; proposed the universal Turing machine model
Wrote the original Unix operating system at Bell Labs; co-developed the UTF-8 encoding
Often considered the first computer programmer; published the first algorithm for Babbage's Analytical Engine
Created the C programming language; rewrote Unix in C
Co-inventor of TCP/IP with Robert Kahn; recognized as one of the "fathers of the internet"
